The direction came while disposing of appeals filed by an IIT-Kanpur alumnus, who is the founder of a non-governmental organisation engaged in student rehabilitation and mental health counselling

title: The legal questions surrounding India’s E20 rollout | Explained
content: As the transition to E20 continues, many questions continue to be discussed legally with courts continuing to address issues relating to consumer rights, liability, warranty and regulatory safeguards on a case by case basis

title: India extends grant support to Maldives for fitness centres across 42 islands
content: On July 19, Memoranda of Understanding (MoUs) were signed by Maldivian Foreign Minister Iruthisham Adam, Indian High Commissioner G Balasubramanian and Maldives' Youth Empowerment, Sports and Fitness Minister Abdulla Rafiu at a ceremony in Male

content: Welcome to the Tamil Nadu Today newsletter, your guide from The Hindu on the major news stories to follow today.

content: Principal Chief Commissioner of Income Tax said the region’s net collection reached ₹41,568 crore up to July 17; Andhra Pradesh recorded 17% growth at ₹6,607 crore

title: ‘New category’: Mahua Moitra slams Centre over ‘traitor bench’ seating arrangement for rebel TMC MPs
content: The TMC leader argued that the traditional layout of the house has been compromised to accommodate the rebel group, creating what she labelled a new parliamentary classification

title: Tamil Nadu Today: Ammonia gas leak toll rises to nine
content: Welcome to the Tamil Nadu Today newsletter, your guide from The Hindu on the major news stories to follow today.

title: The E20 transition: fuel for debate
content: As car and bike owners report clogged injectors, damaged fuel pumps, and contaminated petrol across the country, a look at competing claims on what is driving the complaints, and whether ethanol is really to blame

title: Parliament Monsoon Session updates on July 20, 2026: Opposition's demand for discussion over question paper leaks rocks both Houses
content: Both Houses adjourned for the day without conducting any meaningful business; Opposition parties are protesting for a discussion on question paper leaks and the student protests in Delhi's Jantar Mantar

title: Students of Morarji residential school in Bhadravati taluk get chicken for lunch after TH highlights deviation from prescribed menu
content: The school, run under the Social Welfare Department, had reportedly been denying non-vegetarian food to the children, though it was part of the official hostel menu, due to a custom imposed by local villagers

title: NIA moves Delhi HC against bail granted to Khurram Parvez, Irfan Mehraj; Court agrees to hear today
content: It was alleged that Irfan Mehraj had worked as a researcher with the Jammu Kashmir Coalition of Civil Society until March 2022, while Khurram Parvez served as the coordinator of the organisation

title: Tamil Nadu Today: Speaker sends notices to four ex-MLAs
content: Welcome to the Tamil Nadu Today newsletter, your guide from The Hindu on the major news stories to follow today.

title: Prohibitory orders in Mumbai from July 23 to August 6; cops cite likelihood of public order disturbance
content: The order will not cover gatherings at courts, government offices and local bodies for official work, academic activities in schools, colleges and educational institutions, and assemblies at factories, shops and establishments for normal trade and business

title: Hyderabad stays awake as Spain’s FIFA World Cup triumph against Argentina sparks midnight celebrations
content: Fans watch FIFA World Cup final on a giant screen set up at People’s Plaza in Hyderabad. Several licensed bars, clubs and microbreweries too hosted live screenings.

title: Associations to protest in Chennai on July 22 for unconditional implementation of 33% reservation for women
content: The protest would demand the implementation of women’s reservation without linking it to the Census and delimitation exercise

title: Rally in Vijayapura in support of Sonam Wangchuk’s fast over NEET paper leak, demand for resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan
content: The protestors condemned the police action against the educationist and social activist, and sought his immediate discharge from the hospital

title: Woman arrested for killing newborn granddaughter in Erode
content: Police said she allegedly strangled the newborn to death at a private hospital in Bhavani because the child was the family’s second daughter

title: TVK government in Tamil Nadu faces a fiscal test in balancing populism and prudence
content: “We can have a welfare State only if we work for it and step up production,” said the then Chief Minister C. Rajagopalachari in June 1952

title: Nine newly-elected members take oath in Rajya Sabha
content: The members who took the oath represent the states of Gujarat, Karnataka, Madhya Pradesh, Meghalaya, Mizoram, Rajasthan and West Bengal

title: Delhi riots case: Supreme Court stays HC order granting Pinjra Tod activist Devangana Kalita access to probe documents
content: According to the Delhi Police, Ms. Kalita and several other activists had conspired to trigger unrest in northeast Delhi in 2020 under the guise of peaceful protests

title: The MGR-Vijay parallel: ‘Jana Nayagan’ revives a rare moment in Tamil Nadu history
content: When MGR became Chief Minister, ‘Meenava Nanban’ followed. Now, history repeats with Vijay’s ‘Jana Nayagan’, releasing on July 23

title: Post-mortem report of murdered man confirms multiple deep stab wounds
content: The report confirmed more than 40 wounds on the victim’s body which was recovered from a pool of blood on July 19

title: Bhaktavatsalam’s take on the destruction of documents relating to anti-Hindi agitation in Tamil Nadu
content: He said he was advised that papers on politicians against whom detention orders had been served need not be kept so that they would not be available to the very people who were likely to take charge of the government. When Ministers came to know about officers who had made reports about them, they would get prejudiced, he further said

title: Meenambal Sivaraj: a trailblazer for Dalit rights and women empowerment
content: Meenambal Sivaraj was born on December 12, 1904, in Rangoon, in a family that placed great importance on education and service. One of her most significant contributions was her work with All India Scheduled Castes Federation founded by Ambedkar

title: The Kumbakonam connection of MGR, M.K. Nambyar and M.S. Swaminathan
content: The families of the iconic film star and former Tamil Nadu Chief Minister, the distinguished constitutional lawyer, and the celebrated agricultural scientist hailed from Kerala. But they spent a formative part of their lives in Kumbakonam, known for its rich cultural heritage, before rising to national prominence in politics, cinema, law, and science

title: The 1995 caste reprisals that scarred southern T.N. also touched Communist leader Nallakannu’s life
content: On December 3, 1995, Nallakannu’s 84-year-old father-in-law, A.K. Annasamy, was murdered by a gang while he was asleep at his house in Marudanvalvu village, then part of Chidambaranar district (now Thoothukudi district)

title: Kerala power crisis: Regulatory panel urges KSEB to ‘improve’ electricity demand forecasts
content: Commission notes that any decision on power purchase based on incorrect demand and supply position is likely to result in operational challenges and financial liability that ultimately gets reflected in retail tariff of consumers

title: Congress slams Centre over handling Wangchuk, exam issues; opposes Higher Education Bill
content: The Congress leader alleged that despite repeated question paper leaks, Mr. Pradhan continued in office and remained “unperturbed,” while the protesters were demanding his resignation

title: Dolphin City: Chennai’s dolphinarium that shaped national debate on marine mammal captivity over 25 years ago
content: Hosting a dolphinarium in Chennai turned into controversy as all the three dolphins died, setting off one of the country’s earliest debates on the ethics of marine mammal captivity

title: When a 16-day strike by transport employees and a bus accident on Deepavali shook Tamil Nadu in 2001
content: In November 2001, transport corporation employees’ unions struck work demanding the Tamil Nadu government to increase annual bonus to 20%. During the strike, 28 persons were killed in Thoothukudi district in an accident involving a TNSTC bus allegedly driven by a temporary casual labourer.

title: NC protests in Delhi to demand statehood for J&K, says government's promise remains unfulfilled
content: Opposition PDP leader Iltija Mufti described the ruling NC’s protest as a “lacklustre and deliberately understated event” for not demanding the restoration of Article 370

title: Reassessing Periyar E.V. Ramasamy’s take on Tamil, which he called a barbarian language
content: The Dravidar Kazhagam founder believed that Tamil lacked sufficient literature that would pave the way for human development and cultivation of scientific temper. ‘It is my anguish that Tamil could not become a language of science and rationalism like English,’ he wrote while defending his characterisation of the language

title: CJP Parliament March LIVE Updates: "Protests To Continue," Says CJP After Meeting With JP Nadda
content: CJP Parliament March LIVE Updates: The police had denied the Cockroach Janta Party the permission to hold the protest march and banned large gatherings.

title: Political Row Erupts In Bihar Over Saffron Colour Plan For 500 New Model Schools
content: Chief Minister Samrat Choudhary on Sunday inaugurated 551 model schools, and according to sources in the education department, officials have been directed to paint all the Saraswati Vidya Niketan schools saffron instead of conventional colours such as pink, sky blue or yellow.

title: Opinion: Opinion | The Refugee With No Passport, And The Illegal Immigrant With Indian Papers
content: How can you expect a passport from a refugee? That's the question - posed by the Delhi High Court - that now defines the story of Maung Henry Htoo Aung Linn.

title: Tear Gas, Lathicharge As At Least 20,000 March With CJP To Parliament
content: CJP Parliament March: The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) supporters had announced a "Chalo Sansad" march against alleged irregularities in NEET.

title: CJP Protest: "End Sit-In": Centre's Appeal After "Cordial Meeting" With CJP Leaders
content: "I have requested all the protesters to end their sit-in and assist the administration in restoring normalcy," Union minister JP Nadda said

title: "Violent, Aggressive": China Coast Guard Attack On Filipino Sailor Caught On Camera
content: The assault was captured on video by other Filipino sailors in the hotly disputed Second Thomas Shoal and set off angry condemnations by Philippine officials.

title: Op Sindoor Big Success, Pak Army Tried To Hide Failure: Imran Khan's Sister
content: Pakistan's National Cyber Crime Investigation Agency (NCCIA) has summoned Noreen Niazi, sister of former prime minister Imran Khan

title: Iran Is In "Full-Scale War" With US, Says President Pezeshkian
content: "The most important battlefield today is the economy and the livelihoods of the people," said the Iranian president in a meeting in Tehran with judicial officials.

title: 'Politicians Come And Go': Larry The Cat Welcomes Another UK PM, His 7th
content: Recruited to deal with a rodent problem, Larry arrived at the prime minister's official residence on February 15, 2011, when David Cameron was leader.

title: Khalil Al-Hayya Named New Hamas Chief 2 Years After Yahya Sinwar's Death
content: Born in 1960, Hayya survived an Israeli assassination attempt in Doha last year, though one of his sons was killed in the strike.

title: "Are Surveillance Norms Being Followed?" High Court To Delhi Cops On CJP Protest
content: "At Jantar Mantar, every protest is always recorded... There is no surveillance," Solicitor General Tushar Mehta argued.

title: Class 12 Student Shot Dead On Way To School In Bihar's Samastipur
content: The police said bike-borne criminals shot dead Umesh Thakur's daughter, Sonam Kumari. She was a student of Kasturba Vidyalaya, Pusa Road.

title: Amid Protests In Delhi, Omar Abdullah's J&K Statehood Pitch At Jantar Mantar
content: It is the first time a serving J&K chief minister has taken to the streets to protest for the restoration of statehood.

title: Andy Burnham Becomes UK's 7th Prime Minister In A Decade
content: Starmer made a short farewell speech outside the prime minister's 10 Downing Street residence, saying "my work is done."

title: US Identifies 2 Soldiers Killed In Iran Strike On Jordan. One Of Them Was 19
content: On Saturday, the US Central Command said two service members were killed in Jordan while defending against Iranian ballistic missile and drone attacks.

title: Why Britain Is Getting A New Prime Minister Without A General Election
content: Burnham was the only candidate to secure enough support from his fellow Labour lawmakers to replace Starmer as leader of the governing party. Because Labour holds a majority in government, its leader also becomes the UK prime minister.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Not Just Jets, China Is Developing A '5-Piece' Carrier Set, And India Should Worry
content: China is now the only state serially producing two distinct stealth fighter families simultaneously, with some estimates projecting that advanced-fighter output across all jet programmes could reach 300-400 aircraft annually by 2027.

title: CJP Leaders Meet Union Minister JP Nadda, Submit Letter Of Demands
content: CJP spokesperson Saurav Das in a post on X said that he and another spokesperson, Ashutosh Ranka, met Union minister JP Nadda at his house for 10 minutes

title: Chinese AI Is Catching Up To The US Faster Than Before: Why It Matters
content: A new study suggests China's top AI models are closing the gap with America's best, while a wave of new launches is reshaping the global AI race.

title: 'Got no commitments': CJP leaders handover 3 demands to JP Nadda; minister says end 'sit-in'
content: Cockroach Janata Party leaders met Union minister JP Nadda regarding the NEET-UG paper leak case. Protesters marched towards Parliament demanding accountability and specific government actions. The Union minister sought time to consult leadership on the party's demands. Meanwhile, Union Home Minister Amit Shah met the Education Minister at Parliament. Police used mild force and tear gas as demonstrators attempted to breach barricades.

title: 'Weaponisation of water': Pak seeks Canada's help to revive Indus Waters Treaty with India
content: Pakistan's Deputy Prime Minister Ishaq Dar requested Canadian support for the Indus Waters Treaty.  He stated India suspended the agreement, adding a new dynamic to security.  Dar urged India to end water weaponization and uphold international law.  India maintains its position remains in abeyance due to terrorism.  Pakistan views the Indus as a lifeline and will defend its sovereignty.

title: WATCH: Spain players form guard of honour for Messi after World Cup win
content: Spain secured the FIFA World Cup 2026 title after a hard-fought extra-time victory over Argentina.  The Spanish team formed a guard of honour for Lionel Messi and his teammates.  Ferran Torres scored the decisive goal which handed La Roja their second World Cup.  Messi's quest for a second World Cup title ended as Argentina's reign concluded.  Spain's disciplined defence successfully contained the legendary Argentine captain throughout the match.

title: JPMorgan made 40% AI job cuts in some teams, but CEO is not ‘convinced’ of its results
content: JPMorgan Chase has reduced some jobs by up to forty percent using artificial intelligence.  CEO Jamie Dimon believes AI offers no unique competitive advantage for the bank.  He stated that AI will not significantly increase the bank's profit margins.  The bank is increasing recruitment for AI-related roles while hiring fewer bankers.  JPMorgan Chase reported strong net income and investment banking fees for the second quarter.

title: 'He has shut up everybody': Ravi Shastri defends Rohit after Lord's century
content: Ravi Shastri supports Rohit Sharma, urging management to let him play as long as desired. Sharma silenced critics with a historic century at Lord's in the third ODI. He stated external noise does not affect his focus on team success. Sharma's performance came amid speculation about his future international career. The veteran opener continues to represent India and contribute to team achievements.

title: Airbnb CEO says will keep investing in New York despite Mamdani's rent freeze plan
content: Airbnb and Anthropic are expanding their New York City operations significantly.  This investment occurs despite concerns from business leaders about city policies.  Airbnb purchased a building for its growing New York workforce.  Anthropic will lease a large office building and double its staff.  These expansions highlight continued business activity amidst policy debates.

title: Delhi HC seeks comprehensive medical record on Sonam Wangchuk's condition
content: The Delhi High Court requested comprehensive medical records for Sonam Wangchuk. Authorities must submit all health documents and reports from various hospitals. Doctors treating Wangchuk were directed to appear before the court. This action follows an appeal against a single judge's decision on his hospital transfer. The court is reviewing the government's decision regarding his deteriorating health condition.

title: Ancient sea worm had jaws made of mysterious bio-metal, scientists find
content: Marine worms possess jaws built from proteins and metal ions, creating bio-metals. These bio-metals exhibit high hardness and a specific response to strain. Researchers discovered a size-dependent elasticity in these worm jaws. This phenomenon is similar to traditional metals like copper and silver. Studying these natural bio-metals could inspire new human-made materials.

title: Legendary Indian singer’s voice was among Nasa’s first sounds sent to space
content: Kesarbai Kerkar, an esteemed Hindustani classical singer, now has her mesmerizing voice traveling beyond our planet. Her rendition of 'Jaat Kahan Ho' was immortalized on Voyager 1, symbolizing India's inclusion among 27 nations showcased on the probe. Renowned for her extraordinary vocal gifts and unwavering commitment to her art, Kerkar's music continues to reverberate throughout the stars, leaving an indelible mark in space.

title: US judge to Meta's laid off employees: Cannot stop the company from job cuts
content: A US judge has allowed Meta Platforms to proceed with planned layoffs of 26 employees. These employees had sued the company alleging AI-driven discrimination in job cuts. The judge declined an emergency request to halt the layoffs while claims proceed. He stated the court may reconsider if new evidence emerges about AI usage. Meta denies wrongdoing and states human decisions guided the workforce reduction.

title: Vacant for decades? Why the government can't simply take over abandoned properties
content: In India, the absence of a comprehensive law governing abandoned buildings leads to fragmented regulations across various legislative acts. Municipal authorities hold the authority to inspect and classify buildings as dangerous. Owners are notified to undertake necessary repairs or secure their properties, with demolition a potential option in cases of imminent risk. Both Maharashtra and Delhi enforce mandatory structural evaluations for older buildings.

title: Colby Covington targets ex-UFC middleweight champ after Belal Muhammad clash
content: The scheduled wrestling match between Colby Covington and Belal Muhammad is no longer taking place. After weeks of rumors following their heated brawl at Real American Freestyle (RAF) 11, Covington has now confirmed that the matchup has been called off. Speaking to the Submission Radio, former UFC interim welterweight champion has revealed that he is now looking at different opponents for his next fight.

title: TheMightyBA's Streamer University 2026 controversy explained
content: The controversy at Streamer University 2026 surrounding TheMightyBA intensified when footage surfaced of him patting MeesterKeem's head, triggering a storm of criticism. Ludwig stood by TheMightyBA, suggesting that redemption is possible, drawing parallels to IShowSpeed’s remarkable evolution. With persistent effort and time, creators can address their past errors and forge a path to redemption.

title: 'His retirement will be decided': Mohammad Kaif makes bold statement on Rohit Sharma's future after Lord's heroics
content: Mohammad Kaif backs Rohit Sharma amid speculation about his international cricket future.  Kaif insists retirement is a personal choice, not dictated by public opinion.  Rohit recently scored a historic century at Lord's, silencing critics effectively.  He believes team management support would greatly benefit the veteran opener.  Kaif hopes for an end to unnecessary noise, allowing Rohit to play peacefully.

title: Ram Temple donation row: Trust deploys new cash-counting team, two-shift system
content: The Ram Temple trust has implemented a new cash counting system with two shifts.  This new process is now under the supervision of the State Bank of India.  A new in-charge has been appointed after the previous one was jailed.  This follows an investigation into alleged theft of donation money.  Authorities arrested eight individuals and recovered significant cash and valuables.

title: Messi branded a ‘baby’ after desperate attempt to get Cucurella sent off
content: Messi’s World Cup final frustration sparked controversy after he appealed for action against Marc Cucurella’s mouth-covering exchange during Argentina’s defeat to Spain. While no punishment followed, pundits criticised the reaction as unsportsmanlike. Spain ultimately prevailed through Ferran Torres’ extra-time winner, ending Argentina’s title defence and leaving Messi’s final World Cup appearance marked by disappointment.

title: Quote of the day by Sylvester Stallone: ‘If you know what you're worth...'
content: Sylvester Stallone reminds us that true success is rooted in resilience rather than blind confidence. He advocates for embracing failures while holding firm to our goals. Life’s myriad challenges often test our resolve, but many great accomplishments arise only after we have weathered numerous setbacks and obstacles. Ultimately, it's how we respond to adversity that shapes the path toward achieving our dreams.

title: Gujarat Housing Board’s 100% penalty waiver scheme: Eligibility & how to apply
content: Gujarat's 100% Penalty Mafi Yojana offers a complete waiver of late payment penalties. Beneficiaries of Gujarat Housing Board and Slum Clearance Cell can apply. Applicants must pay all pending arrears in a single lump sum by March 31. This scheme helps beneficiaries clear dues and move towards property ownership. Online applications are now being accepted for this beneficial initiative.

title: WC Final 2026: How Argentina ruined Messi's 'Last Dance'
content: Spain win the World Cup, Argentina win the charge sheet, and Lionel Messi’s final bow turns into football’s dirtiest farewell.

title: A World Cup of records, redemption and remarkable stories: How football won the summer
content: Spain and Argentina fought for the sport's biggest prize right down to the very last kick of extra time, watched by a global audience that dwarfed anything politics could ever hope to command. In the end, the football did what it has always done best, it brought people together, across borders and languages and passports, no matter what was happening outside the stadium gates. That, more than any scoreline, is why this World Cup will be remembered as the biggest and perhaps one of the best ever played inside the lush green grasses with white lines.

title: US fuel prices rise: Gasoline returns to $4 per gallon after Middle East flare-up
content: Average petrol prices in the United States have now reached four dollars per gallon. This increase follows recent flare-ups and rising tensions in the Middle East region. Motorists in several states have been paying more than four dollars for some time. Crude oil prices have risen, impacting global fuel costs significantly. Higher fuel expenses may affect grocery prices and voter concerns during elections.

title: Scott Dureau dies at 39: Former Newcastle Knights star loses cancer battle
content: Former Newcastle Knights and Catalans Dragons halfback Scott Dureau has passed away at the age of 39. He has died after a long and courageous battle with brain cancer. The former rugby league star had been fighting the disease for more than 12 years ever since doctors first discovered a tumour behind his eye back  in 2013.

title: Vikrant Massey says staying friends with an ex is 'red flag': 'We are friends with our exes'
content: Vikrant Massey opened up about his relationship with Sheetal Thakur, highlighting their long friendship that blossomed into marriage. He expressed concern over maintaining ties with ex-partners, deeming it a potential issue for future relationships. Together for over ten years, the couple tied the knot in February 2022 and welcomed their son Vardaan in 2024. Fans can look forward to his film 'Musafir Cafe' debuting on Netflix this July.

title: 'It was written': Pedri's cryptic 13-month-old post goes viral after Spain's WC win
content: Spain midfielder Pedri celebrated their FIFA World Cup 2026 victory with a mysterious social media post.  He had shared a cryptic number sequence over a year prior to the triumph.  This sequence was interpreted by fans as the date of the final match.  Spain defeated Argentina 1-0 after extra time to secure their second title.  Pedri played a vital role throughout the team's successful campaign.

title: From Tejas rejection to 5th-gen fighter jet hopes: Can Kaveri 2.0 finally deliver?
content: India's Kaveri jet engine project shows signs of revival after years of delays. Defence Minister Rajnath Singh urged scientists to develop sixth-generation fighter engines soon. Technologies from the Kaveri program will support future combat aircraft and unmanned vehicles. India continues to rely on imported GE engines for its current fighter fleet. This renewed focus aims for self-reliance in military aviation propulsion systems.

title: 'Don't arm-twist the government': Kangana Ranaut criticises CJP protest near Parliament
content: Kangana Ranaut criticized ongoing CJP protests near Parliament, stating demonstrations should not pressure the government. She believes Parliament is the proper forum for discussing public issues and government decisions. Protests causing disruptions are inappropriate as the elected government has a mandate to govern. Meanwhile, CJP protesters faced heavy security and tear gas as they marched towards Parliament. Several Bollywood personalities have voiced support for Sonam Wangchuk's ongoing hunger strike.

title: Paid for 90g detergent, got only 70g; consumer wins Rs 50,000
content: Hindustan Unilever Limited was found liable for selling an underweight Surf Excel detergent pouch. The state consumer commission reduced the compensation awarded to the original complainant. The case began in 2020 when a consumer found a packet lighter than labelled. HUL's appeal argued against the district commission's jurisdiction and findings. The commission upheld the unfair trade practice finding but lowered the financial award.

title: Indigenous bullet trains: What’s happening on country's first high-speed rail project
content: India is planning to begin trials of its domestically developed bullet trains by the middle of 2027. Civil construction on the 97-km Surat-Vapi high-speed rail stretch is scheduled to be completed this year.

title: Karnataka HC questions 12-year delay in education quota for transgender persons
content: Karnataka High Court questioned the state government for its delayed implementation of transgender reservation in educational institutions. A transgender student's admission denial to NLSIU Bangalore initiated the legal proceedings for this reservation policy. The court emphasized the Supreme Court's 2014 NALSA ruling, which mandated such reservations for transgender individuals. Karnataka already provides reservation in government jobs, making the education sector's delay questionable.

title: Locked flat, confession voicemail: Thane couple booked for murdering woman’s lover
content: A decomposed body was found in a locked Dombivli flat after neighbours noticed a foul smell. The tenant's wife allegedly confessed to the murder via voicemail sent to the house owner. She claimed her lover had been troubling her, leading to the killing by the couple. Police are searching for the absconding couple after registering a murder case.

title: Era of cheap borrowing may be over as world enters 'a new macro regime': Moody's
content: The era of ultra-low interest rates is ending, ushering in higher borrowing costs.  Investors anticipate sustained investment in artificial intelligence and infrastructure projects.  Geopolitical risks are also expected to remain elevated in the coming years.  Bond markets reflect expectations of structurally higher interest rates for an extended period.  Capital will likely flow towards AI, defense, and critical minerals sectors.

title: 'Lacklustre': PDP takes swipe at NC's Delhi protest for J&K statehood
content: Peoples Democratic Party leader Iltija Mufti criticized the National Conference protest for statehood restoration. She deemed the demonstration lacklustre and deliberately understated, lacking a call for Article 370. Mufti argued a Jantar Mantar protest would have demanded special status reinstatement. The PDP stayed away, insisting on Article 370 restoration and prisoner release. This follows the 2019 revocation of Article 370 and state bifurcation.

title: Away from the bell and egg-and-bacon ties, Rohit keeps his England tradition alive
content: In the lead-up to the third ODI between India and England at Lord's, more focus was on Rohit Sharma than the series decider itself. With India's ODI World Cup plans underway, Rohit's form came under fire, and retirement rumours gathered momentum. With Virat Kohli batting at the other end in a monstrous 388-run chase, he decided to shut them down by becoming the first Indian to score a ton at the Home of Cricket.

title: India approves its first dengue vaccine QDENGA after DCGI clearance
content: Dengue is a mosquito-borne viral disease reported in more than 125 countries. According to the company, India accounts for nearly one-third of the global dengue burden, with rapid urbanisation, climate change and widespread mosquito breeding continuing to increase the risk of infection across the country. Vaccine QDENGA is given as two injections of 0.5 ml, administered under the skin three months apart.

title: 'Dhamaal 4' BO Day 11 [LIVE]: Ajay Devgn's comedy reaches Rs 125 crore India net milestone
content: Ajay Devgn's 'Dhamaal 4' continues its impressive second weekend run at the Indian box office. After comfortably crossing the Rs 100 crore India net mark on Day 8 and registering a strong Saturday jump on Day 9, the comedy entertainer has now entered Day 10 with Rs 112.46 crore India net (live). The Indra Kumar directorial is now targeting a Rs 115 crore India net finish by the end of its second weekend, while the worldwide gross is expected to move closer to the Rs 160 crore milestone, despite competition from Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey'.
